message:
fix: use INSERT OR IGNORE for messages to prevent UNIQUE constraint crash on --force-rebuild

The bare INSERT INTO messages failed with UNIQUE(conversation_id, idx) when
--force-rebuild rescanned the filesystem and re-imported conversations already
present in SQLite.  This happened because the fingerprint-based deduplication
can miss edge cases where idx ranges don't fully overlap.

Changed franken_insert_message to:
1. Use INSERT OR IGNORE — silently skips if (conversation_id, idx) exists
2. Return Option<i64>: Some(id) for new inserts, None for skipped duplicates
3. All 6 callers now skip snippets/FTS/bookkeeping for None (existing messages)

The execute_compat return value (rows_changed) cleanly distinguishes new inserts
(1) from ignored duplicates (0), avoiding any last_insert_rowid ambiguity.

Fixes #170

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
